summary: Flat ear‑monitor feed of a 1995 rehearsal that offers rare insight into the band working through “Unbroken Chain,” but the music is unfinished and the sound lacks bass and stereo depth.
review: This recording captures a late‑spring 1995 soundcheck where Phil Lesh runs the band through “Unbroken Chain” (with only brief Jerry Garcia input) and a fragment of “Here Comes Sunshine.” The monitor mix is thin and flat, missing low end and dynamic width, but listeners appreciate the glimpse into the group’s rehearsal process and hear moments of Jerry’s good‑natured playing and a nice take on “Broken Down Palace.”

ai_rating.rationale: Average to above‑average show content (rare rehearsal insight, some praised moments) combined with a consistently flat, low‑bass monitor recording yields an overall rating around the middle of the scale.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Ear‑monitor feed; lacks bass and stereo dynamics, sounds flat and somewhat muffled; no clear soundboard mix.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Rehearsal‑style set dominated by Phil directing the band through “Unbroken Chain,” with Jerry joining only toward the end; overall informal and unfinished, but offers an interesting glimpse of the band’s working process.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Appears late in the session, sounds jovial and happy despite speculation about his health; asked by Phil if he could “butcher” the tune, no audible response.
band_member_comments.Phil: Acts as the de‑facto director, repeatedly telling band members how to play "Unbroken Chain" and guiding the rehearsal.
